title: [StandardToHandshake] Add transformation for feed-forward task pipelining
body:
I'm working on introducing transformation for safe task pipelining in feedforward computational DAGs. This issue should serve as a place to document the progress and discuss the different steps required for this feature. 

@mortbopet Already implemented a task pipelining "guard" for loops that ensures that only one control token is live in a loop. This ensures a correct execution, as it prevents collisions of control tokens. In his [Master's Thesis](https://infoscience.epfl.ch/record/292189?ln=en) it is additionally described how a similar transformation for feed-forward computational graphs could be implemented but it was omitted due to it not being required for his use cases. In my stream project, the generated circuits are continuously fed with new control tokens. Therefore, I observed reordering due to having different branches with different latencies. 

As of yet, I do have test cases that demonstrate the reordering of results due to inputs taking different paths. To simulate this, I created a new test driver in SystemVerilog that feeds multiple inputs to one circuit. It requires some cleanup but should be usable for other test cases. 
Furthermore, I started to implement a transformation that inserts new blocks for all conversion points. Having these blocks already beforehand simplifies the transformations substantially (Figure 6.10 in the thesis). 

As of now, I do see the following steps:
- [x] Add test driver to test circuits with multiple inputs. This can be a separate PR I think.
- [x] Introduce a transformation that inserts explicit merge blocks where needed. Can be a separate PR as well.
- [x] `FeedForwardNetworkRewriter` class, similar to `LoopNetworkRewriter`, which inserts the necessary logic.
